source: Learning bash with Rocky - Data Entry and Manipulations: The read, cut, tr, basename and dirname commands

questions.q: Which command reads user input into a variable?
questions.why: read pauses the script and stores the typed input in a variable.

questions.q: Which read option displays a prompt message?
questions.why: read -p "message" name shows a prompt before reading.

questions.q: Which read option hides the input (e.g. for a password)?
questions.why: read -s hides what the user types.

options: Limits input to 5 characters and validates automatically (no Enter needed)
questions.why: -n limits the number of characters and auto-validates once reached.

questions.q: Which system variable defines the field separator read uses to split words?
questions.why: IFS defaults to space, tab and newline.

questions.q: Which command isolates a column/field from a file or stream?
questions.why: cut isolates columns; often combined with grep via a pipe.

options: -d sets the field delimiter (:); -f selects the field/column number
questions.why: -d is the separator, -f the column number (and -c selects character positions).

questions.q: Which command converts a string, e.g. `tr -s "o"` to squeeze repeated o's to one?
questions.why: tr translates/squeezes/deletes characters; -s reduces the specified char to a single unit.

questions.q: For a path in $FILE, what do basename and dirname return?
options: basename gives the filename, dirname gives the parent directory
basename the parent, dirname the filename
